The delusional Apple consumer electronics OEM template in the original data has been thoroughly eradicated. This is an extremely typical highly subdivided desk address (-7512) in a maker space or co-working office. The logical deduction instantly demotes it: on a rented desk of a few square meters on the 4th floor in Jianye District, the boss, acting as a sole commander, claims to have built an 'electronic equipment manufacturing' assembly line? This is not just a trampling on industrial common sense, but a blatant provocation to the science of floor load-bearing. The probe mercilessly deleted the grand narrative of all 'R&D, manufacturing' in the license, locking onto its only true commercial actions in the latter half of the license: 'system integration; sales; technical services.' The truth is extremely bare-bones: this is a 100% pure 1-Employee Co-working Desk & IT Broker. The boss rented a desk, primarily taking orders online, helping people procure computer accessories and network equipment, or occasionally going on-site to help clients connect the purchased equipment to the network (system integration). The 'major manufacturing plant' tag bestowed upon it by officials has been subjected to the most thorough 'desk-level' demotion by the system.
